overview.value: ITU is the United Nations specialized agency for digital technologies, working towards harnessing innovation and connecting everyone to ensure a better future for all. It was established in 1865 to manage the first international telegraph networks and has been connecting people for over 160 years. ITU is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland, with regional offices on every continent.

focus.value: United Nations specialized agency for digital technologies

events.headline: International Telegraph Union founded in Paris
International Telegraph Union renamed International Telecommunication Union
ITU becomes a specialized agency of the United Nations
ITU holds first conference to allocate frequency bands for space services
ITU's WRC-19 sets regulatory procedures for non-geostationary satellite constellations
ITU's WRC-23 allocates new spectrum for satellite broadband on moving vehicles
